# CDE/PII Tagging: gold-regional-price-parities

**Table:** `consumable.regional_price_parities` (Iceberg: `consumable.regional_price_parities`)
**Spec:** `docs/specs/gold-regional-price-parities.md`
**Parent Specs:** `docs/specs/silver-base-bea-rpp.md`, `docs/specs/raw-ingest-bea-rpp.md`
**Date:** 2026-04-11
**Agent:** @cde-tagger
**Zone:** Gold (Consumable — pure shaping, row-for-row promote)
**Contract:** `governance/data-contracts/gold-regional-price-parities.yaml` *(to be generated by @doc-generator — embed the fragment below)*
**Upstream tagging:**
- `governance/cde-tagging/silver-base-bea-rpp.md` (Silver, 8 CDE, 0 PII)
- `governance/cde-tagging/raw-ingest-bea-rpp.md` (Bronze, 4 CDE, 0 PII)
**Upstream PII scan:** `governance/pii-scans/gold-regional-price-parities.md` (decision: **NO PII**, k-anonymity floor ~584,000 unchanged)

---

## Domain Context Referenced

- `governance/domain-context.md` — BEA Regional Price Parities section and Gold consumable guidance for `consumable.regional_price_parities`
- `governance/domain-context.md` §BEA RPP §Source Codes → Business Concepts — explicit @cde-tagger auto-approval guidance for `rpp_all_items`, `state_fips`/`geo_fips`, `state_name`/`geo_name`, `purchasing_power_multiplier` (cross-refs BT-098, BT-099)
- `governance/domain-context.md` §BEA RPP §Canonical Concept Map — CORE business concepts: State Identifier, Purchasing Power Multiplier, Data Year, Census Region
- `governance/eda/gold-regional-price-parities-eda.md` (via audit trail) — confirmed 51 rows / 15 columns, `cost_tier` distribution across all 4 materialized tiers, `adjusted_Nk` arithmetic verified against spot-check table, inverse-invariant holds
- `governance/pii-scans/gold-regional-price-parities.md` — **NO PII** across all 15 Gold columns; zero-PII claim from Bronze/Silver holds. Grain unchanged at 51 state rows, k-anonymity floor ~584,000 (Wyoming).
- **Applicable regulations:** NONE — BEA RPP is U.S. Government–published aggregate economic statistics in the public domain. No HIPAA, FERPA, GLBA, SOX, GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, or PCI DSS exposure.
- **PII expectations:** NONE — all 15 Gold columns are state-level aggregates, deterministic scalar transformations of state-level aggregates, or pipeline metadata.

---

## Table Context: Why CDE Decisions Matter Here

`consumable.regional_price_parities` is the **business-ready, display-ready Gold reference** for salary purchasing-power adjustment across FutureProof. Every screen that shows a salary number reads from this table, the MCP tools `get_regional_price_parity` and `compare_purchasing_power` return rows from this table verbatim, and the Gemma narrative prompts that soften numeric precision on estimated rows key off columns defined here. This is not an internal analytics table — it is the **consumer-facing Gold consumable**. CDE density is accordingly high: columns that feed the frontend, MCP tool responses, or Gemma's narrative logic are CDE candidates unless they are grain surrogates or batch stamps.

The Gold→Silver relationship is pure shaping:

- **8 columns carry forward from Silver unchanged** (`state_fips`, `state_name`, `state_abbr`, `census_region`, `rpp_all_items`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`, `data_year`) — all 8 were Silver CDEs
- **5 columns are newly derived in Gold** (`cost_tier`, `adjusted_30k`, `adjusted_50k`, `adjusted_75k`, `adjusted_100k`)
- **2 columns are Gold operational/grain** (`record_id` with `rpc:` prefix, `promoted_at` batch timestamp)

Per Brightsmith governance, CDE flags do **not** propagate across zones. Even though every Silver CDE is reproduced here, each one is re-evaluated against its Gold role below. In this case all 8 Silver CDEs are re-affirmed — in Gold their role is even more load-bearing because Gold is the table consumers actually read. The 4 `adjusted_Nk` columns and `cost_tier` are newly evaluated and all flagged as Gold-origin CDEs per the spec's explicit downstream consumption pattern.

---

## Columns Flagged as CDE

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | Origin | Rationale |
|---|--------|------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | `state_fips` | string | **true** | Silver passthrough | **Primary key** of the Gold table and dedup grain for `compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc')`. Join anchor for every MCP tool lookup and frontend state-selector round-trip. ANSI/FIPS external standard. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set; auto-approved per domain-context.md §BEA RPP. |
| 2 | `state_name` | string | **true** | Silver passthrough | Primary human-readable display label served by MCP tool responses (`"state_name": "California"`) and the frontend salary-adjustment UI. Fallback identifier when Gemma or a user passes state by full name rather than FIPS or abbreviation — the MCP tool `get_regional_price_parity(state)` accepts all three forms. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |
| 3 | `state_abbr` | string | **true** | Silver passthrough | The identifier the frontend actually uses. The student's state-selector widget stores a 2-letter USPS code; the MCP tool's `state` parameter accepts it; every chart/display label and URL query string uses it. Without this column the frontend cannot round-trip the user's state selection back to this row. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |
| 4 | `census_region` | string | **true** | Silver passthrough | Four-valued Census Bureau enum driving frontend regional comparison views and the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss's regional cost tiering. Gold DQ re-enforces value domain and completeness. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |
| 5 | `rpp_all_items` | double | **true** | Silver passthrough | **The entire analytical payload of the table.** Every Gold derivation (`cost_tier`, the 4 `adjusted_Nk` columns) and every downstream MCP or frontend salary figure is a pure function of this column. If this value is wrong for a state, every salary figure displayed to every student who selects that state is wrong. BT-098 (Regional Price Parity). Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set; auto-approved per domain-context.md §BEA RPP. **Highest-criticality column in the table.** |
| 6 | `purchasing_power_multiplier` | double | **true** | Silver passthrough | Pre-computed salary adjustment factor (`100.0 / rpp_all_items`). Every Gold `adjusted_Nk` derivation is `N × purchasing_power_multiplier`; every ad-hoc salary adjustment Gemma narrates is a single multiply against this factor. Inverse invariant (`pp_mult × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0`) is a P0 Gold DQ rule. BT-099 (Purchasing Power Multiplier). Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |
| 7 | `cost_tier` | string | **true** | **New in Gold** — derived from `rpp_all_items` via 5-bucket CASE | **Directly consumer-facing classification.** Spec §Technical Design explicitly states: drives **frontend color coding**, **boss-fight difficulty selection**, and **Gemma narrative prompts**. Five-valued enum (`very_high`, `high`, `average`, `low`, `very_low`). Gold DQ is P0 on both value-domain membership and classification correctness (every row's `cost_tier` must match the CASE expression applied to its `rpp_all_items`). BT-106 (Cost Tier, new in this spec). Without this column the frontend has no color key and the boss-fight system has no difficulty input. **New Gold-origin CDE flag.** |
| 8 | `adjusted_30k` | double | **true** | **New in Gold** — `30000.0 × purchasing_power_multiplier`, rounded to 2 decimals | **Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $30K anchor** — one of four values Gemma surfaces to students and the frontend displays on every salary screen. The spec's stated purpose of the Gold layer is to make these values immediately displayable without client-side math. P0 DQ rules pin each value to within 1 cent of the derivation formula and spot-check California and Iowa against the BEA-verified table. BT-107 (Adjusted Salary, new in this spec). **Not** individual earnings data — this is a state-level reference value at a fixed national anchor (confirmed non-PII). **New Gold-origin CDE flag.** |
| 9 | `adjusted_50k` | double | **true** | **New in Gold** — `50000.0 × purchasing_power_multiplier`, rounded to 2 decimals | Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $50K anchor. Same role as `adjusted_30k`: a value Gemma and the frontend display directly. P0 DQ with spot-check assertions for CA (`< 50000.0`) and IA (`> 50000.0`). BT-107. **New Gold-origin CDE flag.** |
| 10 | `adjusted_75k` | double | **true** | **New in Gold** — `75000.0 × purchasing_power_multiplier`, rounded to 2 decimals | Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $75K anchor. Same role and criticality as `adjusted_30k` and `adjusted_50k`. BT-107. **New Gold-origin CDE flag.** |
| 11 | `adjusted_100k` | double | **true** | **New in Gold** — `100000.0 × purchasing_power_multiplier`, rounded to 2 decimals | Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $100K anchor. Closes out the 4-value set the product team selected as common display thresholds; all four are uniformly P0-gated and uniformly consumed by Gemma and the frontend. BT-107. **New Gold-origin CDE flag.** |
| 12 | `verification_status` | string | **true** | Silver passthrough (explicit carry-forward per Bronze Condition 7) | Per-row provenance Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in MCP tool responses (`bea_official` vs. `estimate`). Directly gates whether Gemma says "California's RPP is 110.7" vs. "approximately 108". Bronze staff review Condition 7 explicitly requires Gold to preserve this column; the Gold P0 DQ rule pins `COUNT(*) WHERE verification_status='bea_official' = 8` and an allow-list subset check on FIPS codes. BT-105 (Data Verification Status). Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |
| 13 | `data_year` | int | **true** | Silver passthrough | Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP index is re-published annually; a mis-labeled `data_year` silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure and every salary displayed. Governs the annual refresh replacement semantics when 2025 BEA RPPs publish. P0 DQ pins `data_year = 2024` and `COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1`. BT-102. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set. |

**Total CDE flags: 13 of 15 columns.**

Breakdown:
- **8 carried forward (re-affirmed) from Silver:** `state_fips`, `state_name`, `state_abbr`, `census_region`, `rpp_all_items`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`, `data_year`
- **5 new Gold-origin CDEs:** `cost_tier`, `adjusted_30k`, `adjusted_50k`, `adjusted_75k`, `adjusted_100k`

---

## Columns Flagged as PII

**None.** Per `governance/pii-scans/gold-regional-price-parities.md`, all 15 Gold columns are non-PII:

- The 8 Silver passthrough columns inherit their Silver non-PII classification (state-level public jurisdiction identifiers and macroeconomic aggregates).
- `cost_tier` is a lossy 5-bucket generalization of `rpp_all_items`, which *increases* k-anonymity rather than decreasing it.
- `adjusted_30k` / `adjusted_50k` / `adjusted_75k` / `adjusted_100k` are state-indexed reference values at fixed national salary anchors — **NOT individual earnings data**. Mathematically 1:1 scalar functions of `purchasing_power_multiplier`, which is itself a state-level non-PII aggregate.
- `record_id` (`rpc:<state_fips>`) is a pseudonym for a jurisdiction, not a person — derived from a non-PII input.
- `promoted_at` is a batch-level ETL operational timestamp, identical across all 51 rows.
- k-anonymity floor is unchanged from Silver/Bronze at ~584,000 (Wyoming).

No column receives `is_pii: true`. No sensitivity classification above `public` is required. No RLS, no column masking, no encryption-beyond-baseline.

**0 of 15 columns flagged as PII.**

---

## Columns Evaluated — Not Flagged

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | is_pii | Reason Not Critical / Not Sensitive |
|---|--------|------|--------|--------|-------------------------------------|
| 14 | `record_id` | string | false | false | Deterministic Gold grain surrogate built as `compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc')`. Pure function of `state_fips`, so it encodes no additional information — flagging would be redundant with the `state_fips` CDE. Consumed only by the pipeline's dedup / upsert machinery, not by business consumers, MCP tools, or user-facing displays. Not a decision input. Same rationale as Silver's `record_id` under its own `rpp:` prefix. |
| 15 | `promoted_at` | timestamp | false | false | Gold promotion batch stamp. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch. Replaces Silver's `ingested_at` / `source_load_date` operational pair with a single Gold-zone timestamp. Useful for operational observability and freshness tracking but not a decision input to any downstream product. Freshness is governed by the annual refresh cadence, not per-row timestamp. Not a personal date. |

**2 columns not flagged** — both are pipeline/operational metadata with no business-consumer or MCP/frontend role.

---

## Tag List for Data Contract (for @doc-generator to embed)

When `governance/data-contracts/gold-regional-price-parities.yaml` is generated, embed the following column-level flags. This fragment preserves column order as defined in the spec's 15-column schema table.

```yaml
# Fragment — to be merged by @doc-generator into the gold-regional-price-parities.yaml contract
columns:
  - name: record_id
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Deterministic Gold grain surrogate. Derived as compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). 51 distinct values, one per state."

  - name: state_fips
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-100
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Primary key of the Gold table and dedup grain for compute_grain_id(['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). Join anchor every downstream MCP tool lookup and frontend state selector resolves back to. ANSI/FIPS external standard. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set; auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS state code (e.g., '06' California, '19' Iowa, '11' DC)."

  - name: state_name
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-101
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Primary human-readable display label served by MCP tool responses and frontend salary-adjustment UI. Fallback identifier when Gemma or a user passes state by full name. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Full human-readable U.S. state or jurisdiction name (e.g., 'California', 'Iowa', 'District of Columbia')."

  - name: state_abbr
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-103
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "The identifier the frontend actually uses. Student state-selector widget, MCP tool parameter, chart/display labels, and URL query strings are all built on the 2-letter USPS code. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "2-letter USPS state/jurisdiction abbreviation (e.g., 'CA', 'IA', 'DC')."

  - name: census_region
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-104
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Four-valued Census Bureau enum driving frontend regional comparison views and the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss's regional cost tiering. Gold P0 DQ re-enforces value domain and completeness. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "U.S. Census Bureau region of the state: one of 'Northeast', 'Midwest', 'South', 'West'. DC is assigned to 'South' per Census classification."

  - name: rpp_all_items
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-098
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "The entire analytical payload of the table. Every Gold derivation (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) and every downstream salary figure is a pure function of this column. Wrong values here mis-state every salary adjustment displayed to any student who selects that state. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set; auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section. Highest-criticality column in the table."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "BEA Regional Price Parity index, All Items line code, national baseline = 100.0. Observed range in current load: [86.9, 110.7]."

  - name: purchasing_power_multiplier
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-099
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed salary adjustment factor (100.0 / rpp_all_items). Every Gold adjusted_Nk column is N * purchasing_power_multiplier; every MCP tool response and frontend salary display reads from it. P0 inverse-invariant DQ rule (multiplier * rpp_all_items ~ 100.0) guards computation correctness at rest. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Factor by which a national salary is multiplied to express local purchasing power. Derived as 100.0 / rpp_all_items. DQ range: 0.7 <= x <= 1.3."

  - name: cost_tier
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-106
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Directly consumer-facing classification. Drives frontend color coding, boss-fight difficulty selection, and Gemma narrative prompts per spec Technical Design. Gold P0 DQ enforces both the 5-value enum domain and row-by-row classification correctness against the CASE expression. No substitute column encodes tier. New Gold-origin CDE flag."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Five-bucket classification of state-level cost of living derived from rpp_all_items: 'very_high' (>=108), 'high' (103-108), 'average' (97-103), 'low' (91-97), 'very_low' (<91). Left-closed intervals."

  - name: adjusted_30k
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-107
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $30K national anchor. One of four values the spec identifies as the Gold layer's core purpose: make salary adjustments immediately displayable without client-side math. Directly consumed by Gemma MCP tool responses and the frontend salary screen. P0 DQ pins each value to within 1 cent of the derivation formula. NOT individual earnings data — this is a state-level reference value at a fixed national anchor. New Gold-origin CDE flag."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pre-computed salary adjustment at the $30,000 national anchor. Derived as 30000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to 2 decimals. Reference value, NOT observed individual earnings."

  - name: adjusted_50k
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-107
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $50K national anchor. Same role as adjusted_30k — directly consumed by Gemma MCP tool responses and the frontend. P0 DQ includes California (high-cost) and Iowa (low-cost) spot-check assertions. NOT individual earnings data. New Gold-origin CDE flag."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pre-computed salary adjustment at the $50,000 national anchor. Derived as 50000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to 2 decimals. Reference value, NOT observed individual earnings."

  - name: adjusted_75k
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-107
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $75K national anchor. Same role and criticality as adjusted_30k and adjusted_50k. Directly consumed by Gemma MCP tool responses and the frontend. NOT individual earnings data. New Gold-origin CDE flag."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pre-computed salary adjustment at the $75,000 national anchor. Derived as 75000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to 2 decimals. Reference value, NOT observed individual earnings."

  - name: adjusted_100k
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-107
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $100K national anchor. Closes out the 4-value set the product team selected as common display thresholds. All four adjusted_Nk columns are uniformly P0-gated and uniformly consumed by Gemma and the frontend. NOT individual earnings data. New Gold-origin CDE flag."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Pre-computed salary adjustment at the $100,000 national anchor. Derived as 100000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to 2 decimals. Reference value, NOT observed individual earnings."

  - name: verification_status
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-105
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Per-row provenance Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in MCP tool responses ('bea_official' vs. 'estimate'). Directly gates narrative precision — bea_official rows get exact values, estimate rows get hedged language. Bronze staff review Condition 7 explicitly requires Gold to preserve this column. Gold P0 DQ pins COUNT(*) WHERE verification_status='bea_official' = 8 and enforces an allow-list subset check on the 8 canonical FIPS codes. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Row-level provenance enum: 'bea_official' (8 rows from the February 2026 BEA release) or 'estimate' (43 rows, primary-agent plausible placeholders pending live BEA API load)."

  - name: data_year
    type: int
    required: true
    business_term: BT-102
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP index is re-published annually; mis-labeled vintage silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure. Governs annual refresh replacement semantics. P0 DQ pins data_year = 2024 and COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Year of the RPP estimate. Constant 2024 across all 51 rows in the current load."

  - name: promoted_at
    type: timestamp
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Gold promotion batch timestamp. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch. Replaces Silver's ingested_at / source_load_date operational pair with a single Gold-zone timestamp. Operational observability only."
```

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Columns evaluated | 15 |
| Columns flagged CDE | **13** |
| Columns carried forward from Silver CDE set (re-affirmed in Gold) | 8 (`state_fips`, `state_name`, `state_abbr`, `census_region`, `rpp_all_items`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`, `data_year`) |
| New Gold-origin CDE flags | 5 (`cost_tier`, `adjusted_30k`, `adjusted_50k`, `adjusted_75k`, `adjusted_100k`) |
| Columns flagged PII | **0** |
| Columns not flagged | 2 (`record_id`, `promoted_at` — pipeline grain surrogate + batch stamp) |
| Regulatory frameworks triggered | None |
| Sensitivity classification | `public` across all 15 columns |
| k-anonymity floor | ~584,000 (Wyoming) — unchanged from Silver and Bronze |

**Downstream reminder:** CDE flags do not propagate forward into MCP tool schemas or frontend contracts. When `mcp-bea-rpp` is tagged, it will re-evaluate each MCP response field on its own merits in the serving zone.
